name: For all web UI TUIs (Supervisor agent, standalone, plan mode, and goal agents

rounds.implementation_report:
Implemented Goal `00000SZ3KH6A2G1JKVCWN4Y000` and left it reviewable in commit `624447d3`.

What changed:

- Removed unconditional `scrollToBottom()` after terminal output in [toolbar.js](/home/buddy/projects/refine-next/.git/refine-worktrees/refine-00000SZ3KH6A2G1JKVCWN4Y000-round-1/src/surfaces/web/static/js/features/toolbar.js:1487).
- xterm now follows output while at the bottom, preserves position when the user scrolls into history, and resumes following after returning to bottom.
- Added shared regression coverage for Supervisor, Plan, Goal, and Standalone terminals in [web_supervisor_toolbar.test.js](/home/buddy/projects/refine-next/.git/refine-worktrees/refine-00000SZ3KH6A2G1JKVCWN4Y000-round-1/tests/web_supervisor_toolbar.test.js:243).

Verification:

- Focused terminal suite: `10/10 passed`.
- Complete browser suite: `30/30 passed`.
- Static assets: `54 files`, `static_assets_present: true`.
- `cargo run --manifest-path xtask/Cargo.toml -- check`: passed, including API contract, browser tests, static assets, and runtime layout.
- `git diff --check`, staged diff check, and commit integrity check: passed.
- Initial focused run was `9/10` due solely to cross-realm strict equality in the VM test harness; snapshots were normalized and the rerun passed `10/10`.
- Worktree is clean on `refine/00000SZ3KH6A2G1JKVCWN4Y000/round-1`. No merge or Goal-state mutation was performed.
rounds.prompt: For all web UI TUIs (Supervisor agent, standalone, plan mode, and goal agents), I should be able to freely scroll up and down. Currently, the UI fights this. If I scroll to the bottom, that should stick to the bottom unless scrolled which un-sticks the scroll position. The sticky scroll state is that I can watch what is happening as it happens, but the unsticky state is that I can read what has happen before.
